title: Milkdown plugin for heading-based collapsible sections with React bridge
purpose: Provides collapsible heading sections in a Milkdown editor by integrating React context with ProseMirror decorations and handling state synchronization between the two.
module_context: Exports a plugin key, a factory for the ProseMirror plugin, a NodeView component, and a React hook for bridging the React context with the editor state. Includes helper functions for section ID generation and test utilities.

steps.content: Generate a stable section identifier from the document position of a heading.
Traverse the document to identify all heading nodes and their positions.
For each collapsed heading, determine the range of following sibling blocks to mark as collapsed.
Create decoration nodes for each block in the range and collect them into a decoration set.
Update the shared state reference with all seen section IDs for expand/collapse all functionality.
Initialize a ProseMirror plugin with a key for tracking decorations.
Set up the plugin's state initialization to build initial decorations for the document.
Configure the plugin's apply function to rebuild decorations when document changes or a bump meta is present.
Define the plugin's props to return the current decorations from the plugin state.
Wrap the ProseMirror plugin factory in a Milkdown plugin context.
Return the plugin as part of a flat array of Milkdown plugins.
Access the node and position information from the NodeView context.
Determine the section ID for the current heading.
Register the section with the collapsible sections context on mount.
Render a chevron button that toggles the section's expanded state.
Render the heading content within a container with appropriate classes.
Create a NodeView factory for heading nodes using the provided factory.
Assign the HeadingCollapseView component as the view for heading nodes.
Subscribe to the collapsible sections context to track expanded state changes.
Update the module-level state reference with the current expanded sections.
Increment the version counter in the state reference to trigger recomputation.
Dispatch a meta transaction to the editor view to notify the plugin of state changes.
Reset the module-level collapse state to initial values for testing purposes.
Directly set the expanded sections in the module-level state reference.
Increment the version counter to trigger recomputation.
